candidate_count.note: Mew-X has no comparable creator_candidate surface on the frozen exports.

upstream_opportunity_capture.bound: Cross-source candidate-precision claims remain bounded because Mew-X candidate_precision_proxy is unavailable.

low_slippage_entry.bound: Mew-X is faster after attempt placement, but that does not overturn Dexter's slippage edge on the promoted baseline.

drawdown_containment.bound: This preference is local-path containment only and still assumes timeout-shaped lifecycle behavior is acceptable.

realized_exit_capture.bound: This is an absolute-upside preference, not a peak-retention preference.

stale_risk_avoidance.bound: This is lifecycle containment and does not overturn Mew-X's local drawdown edge.

supported_forms: Use Dexter as the default execution-planning backbone for opportunity capture, low-slippage entry, realized exit capture, and active lifecycle control.
Use Mew-X selectively for strategy variants whose explicit top objective is fast post-attempt fill, minimal adverse excursion, and local-peak retention.
Keep source selection objective-conditioned rather than attempting in-run source fusion.
not_supported: No evidence supports additive gains from fusing Dexter and Mew-X logic inside one execution loop.
No evidence supports transplanting thresholds or trust logic across frozen sources.
No evidence upgrades replay fidelity into a full path-equivalence claim.

decidable_now: Dexter should be the default primary source when objective weights include realized outcome and lifecycle control.
Mew-X should be preferred only when the objective explicitly prioritizes drawdown containment and local-peak retention over realized exit and stale-risk avoidance.
Execution planning can proceed without reopening comparison work.
not_decidable_now: Whether a hybrid implementation would outperform both standalone sources after implementation.
Whether Mew-X would show upstream candidate-quality parity on a comparable creator_candidate surface.
Whether stronger path-sensitive retention claims would survive a path-equivalence review.

residual_and_caveats.replay_scope: Current zero-gap evidence is scoped to close-summary and position_closed realized-return fidelity rather than full execution-path equivalence.

dexter_metric_scope_gap.why_it_matters: A stronger claim about path-sensitive retention still requires a separate lane.

residual_and_caveats.candidate_surface_gap: Mew-X candidate_precision_proxy remains unavailable because the frozen export surface does not expose a comparable creator_candidate path.
residual_and_caveats.strategy_plan_boundary: Dexter is the default primary planning source, but Mew-X remains the bounded primary choice for objectives centered on drawdown containment and local-peak retention.

why: The current surfaces now map the fixed comparison into explicit objective classes rather than only metric-level winners.
Dexter is the default primary choice for the broader operational objective set: opportunity capture, low-slippage entry, realized exit capture, and stale-risk avoidance.
Mew-X still has a real but bounded primary role when drawdown containment and local-peak retention are weighted above realized exit and active lifecycle control.
That is sufficient to begin execution-planning work without reopening comparison or collecting new evidence.
